rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAURICE BASSAN 2004 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Against David Bassan
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now Before the court is Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan's Motion for Entry of Judgment Against David Bassan, brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6. On August 19, 2020, this court entered Order Approving Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release; Instructing That The Trustee Be Governed By the Settlement Agreement to the Extent That Settlement is Inconsistent with the Terms of the Trust and Restatement; Enforcing of Settlement Agreement Under Cal. Civ. Pro. Code section 664.6. The order specified "The Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release attached to the petition filed on 7/8/20 is approved." According the papers before the court on this motion, parties David Bassan and Lou Ann Bassan signed other agreements between themselves: Agreement Between David Bassan and Lou Ann Bassan; Stipulation of Entry of Judgment; Stipulated Judgment (Exhs. B, C, D to Motion). These documents were not part of "the Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release attached to the petition filed on 7/8/20," and so were not considered as part of the Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release submitted for the court's approval, or approved under the August 19, 2020 Order. These documents are outside of the court's powers to enter a judgment under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, as they were not part of the settlement agreement presented to the court for approval. The court's powers to enter a judgment under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 are in practice extremely limited. The judgment must conform exactly to the parties' agreement. The judge may not add provisions or make a new agreement without the parties' consent. (California State Auto. Ass'n Inter-Ins. Bureau v. Sup. Ct (1990) 50 C3d 658, 664.) A judgment entered under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is a ministerial act done according to the terms of the agreement. (Reed v. United Teachers Los Angeles (2012) 208 CA4th 322, 340.) Although the court may interpret terms of a settlement agreement, the court may only render a judgment that is in exact conformity with an agreement or stipulation of the parties. (In re The Clergy Cases I (2010) 188 Cal. App. 4th 1224, 1236; Leeman v. Adams Extract & Spice, LLC (2015) 236 Cal. App. 4th 1367, 1375; Hines v. Lukes (2008) 167 Cal. App. 4th 1174, 1185 & fn.10.) While the court has the authority under the statute to resolve reasonable disputes over the terms of the settlement, it has no authority to impose on the parties more restrictive, less restrictive, or different terms than those contained in their settlement agreement. (Machado v. Myers (2019) 39 CA5th at 790, 792, 794-795.) The court's authority under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is to either approve or disapprove a settlement agreement, not to modify its terms. (Leeman v. Adams Extract & Spice, LLC (2015) 236 CA4th 1367, 1375.) [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Against David Bassan (Added For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] David Bassan is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAURICE BASSAN 2004 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Against David Bassan (Added For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] When there is a dispute as to the terms of an agreement, the court may receive evidence, determine disputed facts, interpret the terms of the settlement agreement, and enter judgment in accordance with the terms of the settlement agreement. (Skulnick v. Roberts Express, Inc. (1992) 2 Cal. App. 4th 884, 889; Fiore v. Alvord (1985) 182 Cal. App. 3d 561, 565-566; Hines v. Lukes (2008) 167 Cal. App. 4th 1174, 1182; Bowers v. Raymond J. Lucia Companies, Inc. (2012) 206 Cal. App. 4th 724, 732.) A settlement agreement must be interpreted according to the principles applied to other written agreements - i.e., to give effect to the parties' mutual intent as it existed at the time, to the extent that this intent is lawful and can be ascertained based on the clear and explicit language of the agreement. (Leeman v. Adams Extract & Spice, LLC (2015) 236 CA4th 1367, 1374.) The court may interpret the terms of the parties' settlement agreement to decide what terms the parties themselves agreed to. (Machado v Myers (2019) 39 CA5th 779, 790). Nothing in Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 authorizes a judge to create the material terms of the settlement. A settlement agreement is unenforceable if the parties fail to agree on a material term or if a material term is not reasonably certain. (Weddington Productions, Inc. v. Flick (1998) 60 Cal. App. 4th 793, 811; Civil Code section 1580, "Consent is not mutual, unless the parties all agree upon the same thing in the same sense".) When ruling on a Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 motion, the court must determine whether the parties entered into a valid and binding settlement, and the material terms of that settlement. (Bowers v Raymond J. Lucia Cos., Inc. (2012) 206 CA4th 724, 732, 734). In doing so, it should consider whether (1) the material terms of the settlement were explicitly defined; (2) the parties understood those terms; and (3) the parties expressly acknowledged their understanding of and agreement to be bound by those terms. (Hines v. Lukes (2008) 167 CA4th 1174, 1182 n6). As with all contracts, the existence of mutual intent is determined by the objective reasonable outward manifestations of intent. In the absence of fraud, mistake, or any other vitiating factor, a party's signature on a written agreement is the party's objective manifestation of assent to the terms set forth in the agreement. (Monster Energy Co. v Schechter (2019) 7 C5th 781, 789.) A judgment issued under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 must conform exactly to the material terms of the parties' settlement agreement. The moving papers draw upon documents and materials not found in the settlement agreement approved by the Court. The relief sought is not rooted in the terms of approved settlement agreement; it is rooted in other materials not included in that agreement (see, e.g. Motion at 2:15-3:12, 3:20-7:7; Exhs. B, C, D to Motion; Spalding Decl. at par. 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, 10; Reply at 1:22, 5:3-16, 6:25-7:9, 8:3-9:19, 10:1-11:20, 14:6-9; Spalding Decl ISO Reply at par. 2, 3; Opposition at 2:25-3:4, 3:18-4:3, 5:16-6:6, 6:11-7:14; Minaise Decl. at par. 3, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11, 13-16; Douds Decl. at par. 2). On this record, the Court declines to act under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, as doing so would require modification of the settlement agreement. The Motion for Entry of Judgment Against David Bassan is DENIED. Neither party shall be awarded fees, nor shall an award of sanctions be made to any party on the basis of this motion. [Part 2 of 3]
